generated_oml:
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Acts as the actuation element for the first axis, driven by a permanent‑magnet synchronous motor."
]

instance SiemensCNCController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller issuing velocity and torque commands to the motor driver."
]

instance MotorShaftEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder attached directly to the motor shaft to capture motor angle and velocity."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncMQTTPayload
]

instance GearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder positioned behind the gearbox to reduce mechanical backlash and enhance positional accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxEncMQTTPayload
]

instance MotorEncMQTTPayload : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorShaftEncoder
]

instance GearboxEncMQTTPayload : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxEncoder
]

instance MotorEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated sequence of motor shaft encoder readings sampled at the control loop rate."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncMQTTPayload
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIngestor
]

instance GearboxEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated sequence of gearbox encoder readings sampled at the control loop rate."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxEncMQTTPayload
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIngestor
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT broker facilitating one‑way data flow from physical to digital twin via structured JSON payloads."
]

instance OPCUAGateway : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "OPC UA gateway ingesting data from PI sensors, PLCs, and edge devices."
]

instance DataIngestor : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component that aggregates incoming sensor data streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIntegrationService
]

instance SimulinkEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink engine executing executable Simulink models for friction and dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService
]

instance CodeGenCPlus : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++ code generator producing real‑time control code from MontiArc/Simulink models."
    DTDFVocab:enables CodeGenerationService
]

instance RelationalStore : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Relational database store for persistence of aggregated data."
]

instance NoSQLStore : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NoSQL database store for high‑flexibility data ingestion."
]

instance TelemetryService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service exposing real‑time motion telemetry to higher‑level applications."
    DTDFVocab:provides AxisPositionInsight, AxisVelocityInsight
]

instance DataIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating sensor traces and preparing data streams for model simulation."
    DTDFVocab:provides AggregatedDataInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing execution of displacement and friction Simulink models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionBehaviorInsight
]

instance CodeGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating deployment‑ready C++ control code from model artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:provides GenerateControlCodeAction
]

instance AggregatedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated insight derived from combined sensor data streams."
]

instance AxisPositionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time insight into the current position of the first axis derived from encoder data."
]

instance AxisVelocityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time insight into angular velocity of the first axis derived from encoder data."
]

instance FrictionBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into frictional dynamics captured by the Simulink friction model."
]

instance GenerateControlCodeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action generating real‑time control code using the CodeGenCPlus enabler."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionBehaviorSimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable Simulink model representing friction behavior on the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngine
]

instance ReducedOrderDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical reduced‑order model of axis dynamics for real‑time control."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngine
]

instance DynamicAxisModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral Simulink model capturing the dynamic behavior of the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngine
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, SiemensCNCController, MotorShaftEncoder, GearboxEncoder, MotorEncMQTTPayload, GearboxEncMQTTPayload, MotorEncoderData, GearboxEncoderData, MQTTBroker, OPCUAGateway, DataIngestor, SimulinkEngine, CodeGenCPlus, RelationalStore, NoSQLStore, TelemetryService, DataIntegrationService, SimulationService, CodeGenerationService, AxisPositionInsight, AxisVelocityInsight, FrictionBehaviorInsight, AggregatedDataInsight, GenerateControlCodeAction, FrictionBehaviorSimModel, ReducedOrderDynamicsModel, DynamicAxisModel
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin lifecycle follows a multi‑layered MBSE paradigm. Initiation begins with requirements capture, aligning operational scenarios with business goals. The functional architecture layer is expressed in SysML using block definition diagrams (BDDs) and internal block diagrams (IBDs), mapping system capabilities. Transitioning to the logical layer, the design leverages architecture description languages (ADLs) such as MontiArc or AADL to specify component–connector models and inter‑component interfaces. The next phase integrates physical simulation, employing MathWorks Simulink or other numerical tools to validate dynamic behavior against mathematical models. At the product/technical layer, implementation details are encoded in source code (C++/Java) or configuration files. Throughout these stages, model transformation tools and version control (e.g., Git) maintain consistency, while governance is provided by MBSE guidelines (RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, SMArDT) that enforce traceability from requirements through to deployed artifacts."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is ensured by calibrating the friction model against reference measurements of the first axis. Deviations between simulated friction losses and measured sensor outputs are quantified; values exceeding predefined thresholds trigger alerts, indicating model drift. Uncertainty sources such as mechanical backlash and temperature effects are considered during calibration, and the corresponding errors are quantified in the validation process. The digital twin employs continuous validation by comparing live sensor data to model predictions, enabling real‑time verification and error detection."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The system uses MQTT as the communication protocol for the one‑way data flow. Beyond the presence of an MQTT broker, no additional details about network topology, bandwidth, latency, reliability measures, redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols are provided in the document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The model adheres to the SysML v1.7 standard and incorporates ADL specifications such as ACME, AADL, and MontiArc. No additional certification or interoperability standard compliance is reported."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin collects real‑time time‑series data from all robot sensors, which are transmitted via the MQTT publish/subscribe protocol. The robot is equipped with an industrial safety cell that physically limits the robot’s working envelope, preventing accidental operator access and defining a maximum working range. Safety status is published over dedicated MQTT topics; an emergency‑stop routine monitors these topics and immediately halts all robot motion when a safety violation is detected. Access to safety topics is restricted by authentication and role‑based access control configured in the MQTT broker, ensuring that only authorized users can subscribe to or publish safety‑critical messages. The twin integrates these safety status signals to provide continuous, real‑time safety monitoring within its simulation environment."
]
